report_name: Energy Sector in Crosshairs: 66% of APT Campaigns Target Utilities, Report Finds

threat_actors.name: Mustang Panda
threat_actors.description: A China-linked APT group identified as one of the most active threat actors targeting the global energy and utilities sector for espionage and reconnaissance.
threat_actors.goals: Strategic intelligence gathering and infrastructure reconnaissance.

threat_actors.name: Volt Typhoon
threat_actors.description: A China-linked APT group targeting the energy and utilities sector, likely for espionage purposes.
threat_actors.goals: Strategic intelligence gathering and infrastructure reconnaissance.

threat_actors.name: Lazarus Group
threat_actors.description: A North Korea-linked APT group identified as one of the most active threat actors targeting the global energy and utilities sector for espionage.
threat_actors.goals: Strategic intelligence gathering and infrastructure reconnaissance.

threat_actors.name: Sandworm
threat_actors.description: A Russia-linked APT group actively targeting the global energy and utilities sector for espionage and reconnaissance.
threat_actors.goals: Strategic intelligence gathering and infrastructure reconnaissance.

threat_actors.name: Stone Panda
threat_actors.description: A China-linked APT group mentioned as actively targeting the energy sector.

threat_actors.name: Hafnium
threat_actors.description: A China-linked APT group mentioned as actively targeting the energy sector.

threat_actors.name: MISSION2074
threat_actors.description: A Chinese-affiliated group noted for conducting the highest number of campaigns against the energy and utilities sector.

threat_actors.name: CyberAv3ngers
threat_actors.description: An Iranian-linked threat group conducting destructive attacks against U.S. critical infrastructure, specifically targeting PLCs.
threat_actors.goals: Destruction and disruption of critical infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Attackers frequently targeted web applications and IaaS environments to gain initial access to energy sector networks.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers compromised cloud accounts to gain access to IaaS environments within the energy and utilities sector.

attack_patterns.name: Active Scanning
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors conducted extensive external reconnaissance, including active scanning, to identify vulnerable systems and services within the energy sector.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: Destructive attacks were carried out using wiper malware like Lotus to cause operational disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: Attacks on OT/ICS equipment, such as those by CyberAv3ngers on PLCs, aimed to have direct physical consequences by abusing remote services.

malware.name: Lotus Wiper
malware.description: A destructive wiper malware used against Venezuela's energy sector to cause operational disruption.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Identified as a living-off-the-land binary to monitor for reconnaissance or lateral movement, especially when initiated by web server processes.

tools.name: wmic
tools.description: Identified as a living-off-the-land binary to monitor for reconnaissance or lateral movement within target networks.

identities.name: Energy and Utilities Sector
identities.description: Global critical infrastructure sector that was the target in 66% of all observed APT campaigns over a three-month period for espionage and reconnaissance.

identities.name: Gazprom
identities.description: Russian energy company whose brand was impersonated in over 34,000 phishing campaigns.

indicators.name: Suspicious Web Application Path Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Web Application Path Access
id: 9b1d8c3a-7e5f-4b2a-8c1d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects access to common web application paths like /owa/, /ecp/, or /api/ which are frequently targeted in exploits against systems like Exchange.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/reports/energy-and-utilities-sector-targeted-in-66-of-observed-apt-campaigns-as-mustang-panda-lazarus-sandworm-remain-active/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-stem|contains:
      - '/owa/'
      - '/ecp/'
      - '/api/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to common web application paths that are frequently targeted in exploits, as mentioned in the hunting hints.

indicators.name: Anomalous PowerShell or WMIC Execution by Web Server
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous PowerShell or WMIC Execution by Web Server
id: f4a3b2c1-d0e9-4f8a-7b6c-5d4e3f2a1b0c
status: experimental
description: Detects living-off-the-land binaries like powershell.exe or wmic.exe being executed by a web server process, which can indicate post-exploitation activity.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/reports/energy-and-utilities-sector-targeted-in-66-of-observed-apt-campaigns-as-mustang-panda-lazarus-sandworm-remain-active/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1047
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_lolbin:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects the use of PowerShell or WMIC for reconnaissance or lateral movement, especially when initiated by web server processes, as suggested in the hunting hints.

indicators.name: Lotus Wiper File Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Lotus Wiper File Creation
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a file named 'Lotus.wiper', associated with the Lotus wiper malware.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/reports/energy-and-utilities-sector-targeted-in-66-of-observed-apt-campaigns-as-mustang-panda-lazarus-sandworm-remain-active/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1485
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\Lotus.wiper'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of the Lotus wiper malware file on critical systems, based on hunting hints.

indicators.name: Suspicious LOLBIN Execution by Name
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Suspicious LOLBIN or Wiper Execution by Name
id: 3d2c1b0a-9f8e-4d7c-6b5a-4f3e2d1c0b9a
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of processes associated with LOLBINs or specific wiper malware mentioned in threat reporting.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/reports/energy-and-utilities-sector-targeted-in-66-of-observed-apt-campaigns-as-mustang-panda-lazarus-sandworm-remain-active/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1047
  - attack.t1485
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\Lotus.wiper'
  condition: selection

relationships.source_name: Anomalous PowerShell or WMIC Execution by Web Server
